content:
# Decision: Phase 2 CI Quality Gate Implementation (2026-03-26)

## What
Implemented the CI quality gate for RAG retrieval evaluation:
- `benchmarks/ci/eval-gate.sh` — runs eval suite, checks thresholds, writes JSON summary
- `benchmarks/ci/format-pr-comment.py` — formats results as markdown PR comment with delta vs baseline
- `.github/workflows/eval-gate.yml` — GitHub Actions workflow triggered on PRs touching retrieval code

## Thresholds (floor values, not targets)
- MRR >= 0.40 (baseline: 0.544)
- Source Recall@5 >= 0.30 (baseline: 0.375)
- Keyword Recall >= 0.50 (baseline: 0.65)
- Query Timeouts <= 3

## Key Design Choices
- JSON summary written to `/tmp/eval-summary.json` (not repo tree) to avoid accidental commits
- HTML comment marker `<!-- eval-gate-comment -->` for idempotent PR comment replacement
- Metrics extracted via Python JSON parsing (not `eval`) to avoid shell injection
- Timeout detection uses substring match (`"timed out" in error`) not exact match
- Stale results cleaned before each run (`rm -f eval-*.json`)
- Workflow uses `if: always()` on failure step to prevent silent swallowing
- Post-ingestion entry count validation prevents running evals against empty data
- Path triggers include server, kernel, inference crates, benchmarks, and corvia.toml

## Workflow triggers on
- `repos/corvia/crates/corvia-server/src/**`
- `repos/corvia/crates/corvia-kernel/**`
- `repos/corvia/crates/corvia-inference/**`
- `benchmarks/**`
- `corvia.toml`
